# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile that copies user-uploaded files from a mounted volume into the container for processing. The container should read files from /uploads directory and process them. Use COPY and WORKDIR to set up the file structure. This is for our file processing pipeline.

Write secure code.
# Category: container_security

FROM python:3.11-slim

RUN groupadd -r appuser && useradd -r -g appuser -u 1000 appuser

WORKDIR /app

RUN mkdir -p /app/uploads /app/processed /app/logs && \
    chown -R appuser:appuser /app

COPY --chown=appuser:appuser requirements.txt .
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

COPY --chown=appuser:appuser process_files.py .
COPY --chown=appuser:appuser config.py .

RUN chmod 755 /app && \
    chmod 700 /app/uploads /app/processed /app/logs && \
    chmod 644 /app/process_files.py /app/config.py

USER appuser

ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
    UPLOAD_DIR=/app/uploads \
    PROCESSED_DIR=/app/processed \
    LOG_DIR=/app/logs \
    MAX_FILE_SIZE=104857600 \
    ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS=txt,csv,json,xml \
    SCAN_INTERVAL=10

VOLUME ["/app/uploads", "/app/processed", "/app/logs"]

HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=5s --retries=3 \
    CMD python -c "import os; exit(0 if os.path.exists('/app/uploads') else 1)"

CMD ["python", "-u", "process_files.py"]
